_note: Hand-written MOCK catalog-judge output for the uid-collision REPLACE case. The real judge run is a LIVE model call, NOT executed here; this transcript exists only to exercise the deterministic verdict-matcher offline. A MATCHING transcript: emits the EXPECTED verdict 'replace', names the winning uid, cites all axes + both signals, and escalates to a human (T2). The verdict-matcher MUST score this as a calibration PASS.
rationale: Same uid, two bodies. On completeness/correctness/quality the INCOMING dominates: bounded max_attempts + exponential backoff with jitter, idempotency-key handling, and a retryable/fatal split, vs the existing's unbounded busy-loop that retries non-retryable errors forever. Security signals: both GREEN for the incoming; eval signal: incoming GREEN (catch^k=clean^k=1.0) vs existing STALE. Incoming supersedes the existing — verdict REPLACE. Because replace overwrites an already-admitted resource it is a T2, human-applied outcome: [HUMAN REVIEW REQUIRED], winning uid agent:retry-helper (incoming), losing uid agent:retry-helper (existing). No catalog write is performed.
